mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding factory output and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes do not coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, restrictive borrowing costs dominate earnings outlooks, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal aggressive monetary tightening into inflation, jointly compressing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of coincident months.

mechanism_narrative: In high labor-slack regimes where unemployment is elevated, month-over-month payroll gains signal accelerating re-employment and consumption recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases while UNRATE is above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand and immediate revenue expansion for consumer-facing firms, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes do not coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of historical months.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated expansion in real GDP and housing starts reflects broad-based demand strength and construction momentum, which jointly lifts equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in GDPC1 and HOUST fail to produce positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of coincident quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 4.0%, tight labor markets signal strong demand for workers and wage-driven consumption power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a rising federal funds rate alongside a rising unemployment rate signals a policy-induced economic slowdown, which together depress corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and UNRATE do not produce negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of coincident months.
